title: tiangolo's boring personal website
text:
Hey! I'm @tiangolo (Sebastián Ramírez). 👋
You are probably looking for my open source projects.
I'm a software developer from Colombia. 🇨🇴
I currently live in Berlin, Germany. 🇩🇪
I created FastAPI, Typer, SQLModel, Asyncer, and a bunch of other open source tools. 🚀
I have been building APIs and tools for Machine Learning and data systems, in the Americas, the Middle East, and Europe, with different teams and organizations. 🌎
I'm now working full time on my open source projects and the ecosystem. 🤓
summary_hint:
I'm @tiangolo (Sebastián Ramírez). 👋
You are probably looking for my open source projects. I'm a software developer from Colombia.

title: Andrej Karpathy
text:
OpenAI where I built a new team working on midtraining and synthetic data generation.
2015 - 2017
I was a research scientist and a founding member at
OpenAI.
2011 - 2015
My PhD was focused on convolutional/recurrent neural networks and their applications in computer vision, natural language processing and their intersection. My adviser was
Fei-Fei Li at the Stanford Vision Lab and I also had the pleasure to work with
Daphne Koller,
Andrew Ng,
Sebastian Thrun and
Vladlen Koltun along the way during the first year rotation program.
I designed and was the primary instructor for the first deep learning class Stanford -
CS 231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition. The class became one of the largest at Stanford and has grown from 150 enrolled in 2015 to 330 students in 2016, and 750 students in 2017.
Along the way I squeezed in 3 internships at (baby) Google Brain in 2011 working on learning-scale unsupervised learning from videos, then again in Google Research in 2013 working on large-scale supervised learning on YouTube videos, and finally at DeepMind in 2015 working on the deep reinforcement learning team with
Koray Kavukcuoglu and
Vlad Mnih.
2009 - 2011
MSc at the University of British Columbia where I worked with
Michiel van de Panne on learning controllers for physically-simulated figures (i.e., machine-learning for agile robotics but in a physical simulation).
2005 - 2009
BSc at the University of Toronto with a double major in computer science and physics and a minor in math. This is where I first got into deep learning, attending
Geoff Hinton's class and reading groups.
summary_hint:
OpenAI where I built a new team working on midtraining and synthetic data generation. 2015 - 2017
I was a research scientist and a founding member at
OpenAI. 2011 - 2015
My PhD was focused on convolutional/recurrent neural networks and their applications in computer vision, natural language processing and their intersection.

title: Tom Mitchell's Home Page
text:
How will they impact us all? As a longtime researcher in AI, I'm excited about the ways in
which these new AI systems can improve our healthcare, education, climate and more. At the same
time, we need to think carefully about how to use them and how to prevent their misuse.
I oppose recent suggestions that we simply ban their development, but I support and participate in
efforts to anticipate their possible uses and impacts, and to help our leaders think through how
governments should react.
It will be useful to regulate AI, but primarily at the application level. For example, AI
applications to medical diagnosis should be regulated very differently from AI applications to
self-driving cars.
U.S. National Academies report
on AI and the Future of
Work, study co-chairs Tom Mitchell and Erik Brynjolfsson, November 2024.
A short fireside chat I had
with Reid Hoffman, moderated by Ryan Heath about what is next for Generative AI,
from the SCSP Global Emerging Technology Summit, Washington D.C., September 21, 2023.
How can AI improve education? Watch my 2025 UCL Peter Kirstein lecture: Where Can AI Take Education by
2030?. My thesis: this is the decade when AI will truly revolutionize online education. Why?
Because (1) for the first time we have finally have online education sites that have taught
millions of students, providing more training data showing how students learn, than a human
teacher could ever see in a 100-year teaching career, and (2) recent AI advances in machine
learning, large natural language models, and reasoning give us the right tools to build AI agents
that tutor online students. ...
Here are some problems I've been working on with my collaborators:
Using Large Language Models like GPT-4 to build new online education systems.
Learning which teaching actions produce best learning outcomes. In January 2023 we deployed
our trained model on the CK12.org platform, and it has
now been used well over a million times to choose the right hint for students struggling to
answer specific questions.
How does the brain represent language meaning? We collect images of brain activity while
human subjects read text, then use machine learning to analyze how observed brain activity
represents language meaning.
...
summary_hint:
How will they impact us all? As a longtime researcher in AI, I'm excited about the ways in
which these new AI systems can improve our healthcare, education, climate and more. At the same
time, we need to think carefully about how to use them and how to prevent their misuse.

title: Thomas Friedel - GitHub
text:
GitHub Profile: Thomas Friedel (tfriedel)
Bio: Machine Learning Engineer
Company: PEAT
Location: Berlin
Top Languages: Python, Java, C

Top Projects:
- trackanalyzer (61 stars, Java): java command line tool for estimating musical key and bpm of digital recordings, to aid DJs in harmonic mixing.
- neural-image-assessment (21 stars, Python): Implementation of NIMA: Neural Image Assessment in Keras
- python-lightify (18 stars, Python): Python module for Osram lightify. This is a work in progress.
- foobar2000-export (14 stars, Java): I reverse engineered the foobar2000 database and playlist formats. You can export to m3u playlists for example.
- tweethoarder (3 stars, Python): dumps twitter likes & bookmarks
- blensor (2 stars, C): Blender Sensor Simulation
- claude-office-skills (438 stars, Python): Office document creation and editing skills for Claude Code - PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, and PDF workflows with automation support
- antigravity_prompts (25 stars): Reverse-engineered prompts and analysis from Antigravity (Google's VS Code fork with AI)

title: Amir Masoud Sefidian - GitHub
text:
GitHub Profile: Amir Masoud Sefidian (iamirmasoud)
Bio: Machine Learning Engineer
Company: Vinted
Location: Berlin
Top Languages: Python, Jupyter Notebook

Top Projects:
- image_captioning (23 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Automatic Image Captioning using PyTorch on MS COCO dataset
- bulkboto3 (19 stars, Python): Python package for fast and parallel transferring a bulk of files to S3 based on boto3!
- facial_keypoint_detection (7 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Facial Keypoint Detection using PyTorch
- pyspark_tutorials (6 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Machine Learning for Big Data using PySpark with real-world projects
- amazon_sentiment (5 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Sentiment Analysis on Amazon Reviews Dataset in PyTorch
- graph_slam (2 stars, Jupyter Notebook): 2D Landmark Detection & Robot Tracking using Offline and Online Graph SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping)
- energy_consumption_prediction (69 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Energy consumption prediction using LSTM/GRU networks in PyTorch
- image_deduplicator (5 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Find and remove duplicated or very similar images from a directory using PyTorch

title: David S. Batista - GitHub
text:
GitHub Profile: David S. Batista (davidsbatista)
Bio: Software Developer | NLP Engineer | Machine Learning @deepset-ai 
Company: @deepset-ai
Location: Berlin, Germany
Top Languages: Python

Top Projects:
- Annotated-Semantic-Relationships-Datasets (705 stars): A collections of public and free annotated datasets of relationships between entities/nominals (Portuguese and English)
- NER-datasets (345 stars, Python): Datasets to train supervised classifiers for Named-Entity Recognition in different languages (Portuguese, German, Dutch, French, English)
- MantisAI/nervaluate (207 stars, Python): Full named-entity (i.e., not tag/token) evaluation metrics based on SemEval’13
- Snowball (178 stars, Python): Implementation with some extensions of the paper "Snowball: Extracting Relations from Large Plain-Text Collections" (Agichtein and Gravano, 2000)
- BREDS (142 stars, Python): "Bootstrapping Relationship Extractors with Distributional Semantics" (Batista et al., 2015) in EMNLP'15 - Python implementation
- haystack-retrieval (7 stars, Python): Different retrieval techniques implemented in Haystack - presented at PyCon Lithuania 2025 🇱🇹
- NER-Evaluation (222 stars, Python): An implementation of a full named-entity evaluation metrics based on SemEval'13 Task 9 - not at tag/token level but considering all the tokens that are part of the named-entity
- Aspect-Based-Sentiment-Analysis (133 stars, Python): Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis Experiments
summary_hint: Software Developer | NLP Engineer | Machine Learning @deepset-ai

title: Tobias Sterbak - GitHub
text:
GitHub Profile: Tobias Sterbak (tsterbak)
Bio: Data Scientist, Mathematician, Machine Learning Engineer, Open Source developer
Location: Berlin, Germany
Top Languages: Python, Jupyter Notebook

Top Projects:
- openandroidinstaller-dev/openandroidinstaller (512 stars, Python): Makes installing alternative Android distributions nice and easy.
- promptmage (115 stars, Python): simplifies the process of creating and managing LLM workflows.
- biaslyze-dev/biaslyze (39 stars, Jupyter Notebook): The NLP Bias Identification Toolkit
- pydataberlin-2019 (39 stars, Jupyter Notebook): This Repository contains the material for my tutorial "Managing the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle with MLFlow" at pyData/pyCon Berlin 2019.
- pydataberlin-2022 (22 stars, Jupyter Notebook): This Repository contains the material for the tutorial "Introduction to MLOps with MLflow" held at pyData/pyCon Berlin 2022.
- keras_attention (29 stars, Python): Contains an implementation of the attention mechanism and a keras text classifier wrapper.
- keras_tta (22 stars, Python): Simple test time augmentation (TTA) for keras python library.
- kernel_eca (13 stars, Python): Scikit-learn compatible Kernel Entropy Component Analysis in Python

title: bogdankostic - GitHub
text:
GitHub Profile: bogdankostic (bogdankostic)
Bio: Machine Learning Engineer
Location: Berlin, Germany
Top Languages: MDX, Jupyter Notebook, Python

Top Projects:
- deepset-ai/haystack (24600 stars, MDX): Open-source AI orchestration framework for building context-engineered, production-ready LLM applications. Design modular pipelines and agent workflows with explicit control over retrieval, routing…
- notion-exporter (19 stars, Python): 🚀 A Python package to seamlessly export your Notion pages to Markdown!
- mmonimon/mensa-skill (4 stars, Python): This skill for Alexa provides information on the menu of German canteens.
- Coreference-Resolution (4 stars, Python): A mention-pair coreference resolution system implemented in Python3.
- TextClassification (0 stars, Python): A Python Library for simple text classification
- fabiomolignoni/KDI-transport (0 stars, Python): Course project of "Knowledge and Data Integration" at UNITN - A.Y. 2019-2020
- llm-prompt-perturbation (4 stars, Jupyter Notebook): Code accompanying the paper “Same Meaning, Different Scores: Lexical and Syntactic Sensitivity in LLM Evaluation”
- notion-haystack (1 stars, Python): 📓 A Haystack component to extract Notion pages to Documents.
